question: [File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.consult / TorusBuyAndProcess.getTorusQuoteForTitanX / TorusBuyAndProcess._swapTitanXForTorus] Can an unprivileged attacker who observes that the TORUS/TITAN_X pool has observationCardinality==1 (so getOldestObservationSecondsAgo returns a value of only a few seconds) manipulate the pool spot price in the same block, then call swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn(deadline), causing consult() to use a near-spot TWAP window that reflects the manipulated price, setting amountOutMinimum to near-zero, violating the invariant that protocol swap output must be within titanXToTorusSlippage% of fair market value, causing scoped impact: attacker sandwiches the swap and extracts TitanX/ETH value from the protocol's burn allocation? Local proof idea: fork-test on mainnet block 25530134 with vm.warp to advance time, mock pool cardinality to 1, manipulate slot0 sqrtPriceX96, call swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn, and assert torusBurnt < expectedMinimumAtFairPrice.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.consult / TorusBuyAndProcess.getTitanXQuoteForETH / TorusBuyAndProcess._swapETHForTitanX] Can an unprivileged attacker who observes that TITAN_X_ETH_POOL (0xc45A81BC23A64eA556ab4CdF08A86B61cdcEEA8b) has a very short oldest observation window manipulate the ETH/TitanX spot price, then call swapETHForTorusAndBurn(deadline), causing getTitanXQuoteForETH to return a near-zero amountOutMinimum for the ETH→TitanX leg, violating the invariant that adjustedTitanXAmount >= fair_market_titanX * (100 - ethToTitanXSlippage) / 100, causing scoped impact: attacker front-runs the ETH→TitanX swap and extracts ETH from the protocol's burn ETH allocation? Local proof idea: fork-test, set TITAN_X_ETH_POOL observation cardinality to 1 via increaseObservationCardinalityNext, manipulate pool price, call swapETHForTorusAndBurn, assert titanXReceived < expectedMinimum.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.getOldestObservationSecondsAgo / TorusCreateAndStake.getEthAmountForTitanX / TorusCreateAndStake._distributeFees] Can an unprivileged attacker who manipulates the TITAN_X_ETH_POOL TWAP (by trading in the pool to depress the TitanX/ETH price) call createTorus(power, lengthInDays) with ETH payment, causing getEthAmountForTitanX(costTitanX) to return a requiredETH far below fair market value, violating the invariant that requiredETH >= costTitanX * fair_ETH_per_TitanX, causing scoped impact: attacker creates Torus positions at a fraction of the intended ETH cost, draining protocol fee revenue? Local proof idea: fork-test, manipulate TITAN_X_ETH_POOL tick downward via large swap, call createTorus with ETH, assert msg.value accepted < expected_fair_cost.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.getOldestObservationSecondsAgo / TorusCreateAndStake.getEthAmountForTitanX / TorusCreateAndStake._distributeFees] Can an unprivileged attacker who manipulates the TITAN_X_ETH_POOL TWAP call stakeTorus(torusAmount, stakingDays) with ETH payment, causing getEthAmountForTitanX(costTitanX) to return a requiredETH near zero, violating the invariant that staking fees must equal 5% of the fair creation cost, causing scoped impact: attacker stakes Torus for free or near-free, gaining staking rewards without paying the intended fee? Local proof idea: fork-test, depress TITAN_X_ETH_POOL price, call stakeTorus with minimal ETH, assert costETH stored in stakePos < expected_5pct_fee.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.consult / TorusBuyAndProcess.getTorusQuoteForTitanX] Can an unprivileged attacker who observes that getOldestObservationSecondsAgo on the TORUS/TITAN_X pool returns 0 (pool initialized in the same block, observationTimestamp == block.timestamp) trigger a call to getTorusQuoteForTitanX which then calls consult(pool, 0), causing consult to revert with 'BP' (require secondsAgo != 0), violating the invariant that swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn must always be callable when liquidityAdded==true, causing scoped impact: attacker permanently DoS-es the burn flow by initializing a competing pool or manipulating cardinality, then extracts value by being the only party able to call the function after recovery? Local proof idea: unit test with mock pool returning observationTimestamp==block.timestamp, call getTorusQuoteForTitanX, assert revert.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.consult (harmonicMeanLiquidity) / TorusBuyAndProcess.getTorusQuoteForTitanX] Can an unprivileged attacker who drains all liquidity from the TORUS/TITAN_X pool during the TWAP observation window cause secondsPerLiquidityCumulativesDelta to equal 0, triggering a division-by-zero revert in the harmonicMeanLiquidity calculation of consult(), violating the invariant that getTorusQuoteForTitanX must return a valid quote whenever the pool exists, causing scoped impact: all swap-and-burn and swap-and-build functions revert, locking protocol ETH and TitanX allocations while the attacker profits from the resulting price impact in the pool? Local proof idea: fork-test, remove all liquidity from TORUS/TITAN_X pool, call getTorusQuoteForTitanX, assert revert with division-by-zero.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.consult (arithmeticMeanTick int24 cast) / TorusBuyAndProcess.getTorusQuoteForTitanX] Can an unprivileged attacker who pushes the TORUS/TITAN_X pool tick to an extreme value cause tickCumulativesDelta / secondsAgoInt56 to produce a value outside the int24 range (-8388608 to 8388607), causing the silent truncation int24(tickCumulativesDelta / secondsAgoInt56) to yield a wildly incorrect arithmeticMeanTick, violating the invariant that arithmeticMeanTick must represent the true TWAP tick within the valid Uniswap tick range [-887272, 887272], causing scoped impact: getTorusQuoteForTitanX returns a grossly incorrect quote, setting amountOutMinimum to near-zero and enabling sandwich extraction? Local proof idea: fuzz test with tickCumulativesDelta values that produce quotients outside int24 range, assert int24 cast matches expected tick.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.getQuoteForSqrtRatioX96 (precision branch) / TorusBuyAndProcess.getTorusQuoteForTitanX] Can an unprivileged attacker who manipulates the TORUS/TITAN_X pool price to a range where sqrtRatioX96 > type(uint128).max cause getQuoteForSqrtRatioX96 to use the lower-precision ratioX128 branch (mulDiv(sqrtRatioX96, sqrtRatioX96, 1<<64)) instead of the ratioX192 branch, producing a systematically lower quote than the true price, violating the invariant that getQuoteForSqrtRatioX96 must return a quote within acceptable precision of the true price regardless of sqrtRatioX96 magnitude, causing scoped impact: amountOutMinimum in _swapTitanXForTorus is set below fair value, enabling sandwich extraction? Local proof idea: unit test with sqrtRatioX96 = type(uint128).max and sqrtRatioX96 = type(uint128).max + 1, compare outputs and assert precision difference < 1 wei.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.getQuoteForSqrtRatioX96 (precision branch boundary) / TorusBuyAndProcess.getTitanXQuoteForETH] Can an unprivileged attacker who manipulates the TITAN_X_ETH_POOL price to exactly sqrtRatioX96 == type(uint128).max cause getQuoteForSqrtRatioX96 to use the ratioX192 branch, while a price of type(uint128).max + 1 uses the ratioX128 branch, creating a discontinuous jump in the returned quote at the boundary, violating the invariant that the quote function must be monotonically consistent across the boundary, causing scoped impact: attacker exploits the discontinuity to obtain a lower amountOutMinimum in _swapETHForTitanX, enabling sandwich extraction of ETH from the burn allocation? Local proof idea: differential test comparing getQuoteForSqrtRatioX96 output at type(uint128).max vs type(uint128).max+1 with same baseAmount, assert no discontinuous jump.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.getOldestObservationSecondsAgo / TorusBuyAndProcess.getTorusQuoteForTitanX] Can an unprivileged attacker who calls increaseObservationCardinalityNext on the TORUS/TITAN_X pool to increase cardinality, causing the next observation slot to be uninitialized, trigger getOldestObservationSecondsAgo to fall back to observations[0] (which has a very old timestamp), setting secondsAgo to a value larger than the pool's actual observation history, causing consult() to call pool.observe() with a secondsAgo that reverts because the pool cannot interpolate that far back, violating the invariant that getTorusQuoteForTitanX must not revert when the pool has valid observations, causing scoped impact: all burn and build swap functions revert, locking protocol funds? Local proof idea: fork-test, call increaseObservationCardinalityNext(200) on TORUS/TITAN_X pool, call getTorusQuoteForTitanX, assert revert.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.consult / TorusBuyAndProcess.getTorusQuoteForTitanX / TorusBuyAndProcess.swapTitanXForTorusAndBuild] Can an unprivileged attacker who manipulates the TORUS/TITAN_X pool TWAP to show a very low Torus price call swapTitanXForTorusAndBuild(deadline), causing _swapTitanXForTorus to set amountOutMinimum to near-zero, then sandwich the swap to receive far less Torus than fair value, violating the invariant that torusPurchased in the build flow must be >= fair_market_torus * (100 - titanXToTorusSlippage) / 100, causing scoped impact: attacker extracts TitanX value from the build allocation by front-running the Torus purchase? [File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.consult / TorusBuyAndProcess.getTitanXQuoteForETH / TorusBuyAndProcess.swapETHForTorusAndBuild] Can an unprivileged attacker who manipulates the TITAN_X_ETH_POOL TWAP to show a very low TitanX price call swapETHForTorusAndBuild(deadline), causing _swapETHForTitanX to set amountOutMinimum to near-zero, then sandwich the ETH→TitanX swap, violating the invariant that titanXAmount received must be >= fair_market_titanX * (100 - ethToTitanXSlippage) / 100, causing scoped impact: attacker extracts ETH from the build ETH allocation by front-running the ETH→TitanX swap? [File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.getOldestObservationSecondsAgo / TorusBuyAndProcess.getTorusQuoteForTitanX] Can an unprivileged attacker who observes that the TORUS/TITAN_X pool was just created (liquidityAdded just became true) and has only 1 observation slot (cardinality=1) call swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn in the same block as a large price-moving trade, causing getOldestObservationSecondsAgo to return a value of 0 or 1 second, making the TWAP essentially the current spot price, violating the invariant that the oracle must use at least a 15-minute TWAP window when available, causing scoped impact: attacker manipulates spot price and sandwiches the burn swap to extract TitanX from the protocol? [File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.consult / TorusCreateAndStake.getEthAmountForTitanX] Can an unprivileged attacker who uses a flash loan to temporarily move the TITAN_X_ETH_POOL tick downward by a large amount, then calls createTorus(power=1000000, lengthInDays=88) with ETH payment in the same transaction, causing getEthAmountForTitanX to return a requiredETH that is a fraction of the true cost, violating the invariant that ETH paid for createTorus must equal costTitanX * fair_ETH_per_TitanX, causing scoped impact: attacker creates maximum-power Torus positions at near-zero ETH cost, gaining disproportionate staking rewards? [File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.getQuoteForSqrtRatioX96 / TorusBuyAndProcess.getTorusQuoteForTitanX] Can an unprivileged attacker who manipulates the TORUS/TITAN_X pool price to a tick where TickMath.getSqrtRatioAtTick returns sqrtRatioX96 = 1 cause getQuoteForSqrtRatioX96 to compute ratioX192 = 1, then Math.mulDiv(1, baseAmount, 1<<192) = 0 for any baseAmount < 2^192, returning a quote of 0, violating the invariant that getTorusQuoteForTitanX must return a positive quote for any positive baseAmount, causing scoped impact: amountOutMinimum in _swapTitanXForTorus becomes 0, removing all slippage protection and enabling full sandwich extraction? [File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.consult (secondsAgoInt56 cast) / TorusBuyAndProcess.getTorusQuoteForTitanX] Can an unprivileged attacker who triggers a state where secondsAgo is set to a value that when cast to int56 (int56(uint56(secondsAgo))) produces a negative value (if secondsAgo > 2^55) cause the division tickCumulativesDelta / secondsAgoInt56 to produce an incorrect sign, yielding a wildly wrong arithmeticMeanTick, violating the invariant that arithmeticMeanTick must have the correct sign and magnitude, causing scoped impact: getTorusQuoteForTitanX returns an inverted or extreme price, setting amountOutMinimum to 0 or an extreme value? [File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.getOldestObservationSecondsAgo / TorusBuyAndProcess.getTorusQuoteForTitanX] Can an unprivileged attacker who observes that the TORUS/TITAN_X pool's observationIndex wraps around (observationIndex = observationCardinality - 1, so next index = 0) and observations[0] is uninitialized cause getOldestObservationSecondsAgo to fall back to observations[0] with timestamp=0, returning secondsAgo = block.timestamp (a very large value ~1784022600), causing consult() to call pool.observe([1784022600, 0]) which reverts because the pool has no observations that old, violating the invariant that getTorusQuoteForTitanX must not revert for a live pool, causing scoped impact: all swap functions revert, locking protocol funds? [File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.consult / TorusBuyAndProcess.getTorusQuoteForTitanX / TorusBuyAndProcess.swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn] Can an unprivileged attacker who repeatedly calls swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn across multiple intervals while maintaining a manipulated TWAP (by continuously trading in the TORUS/TITAN_X pool) extract a cumulative excess incentive fee (1.5% of each interval's allocation) beyond what they would receive at fair prices, violating the invariant that the incentive fee must be computed on the true market value of the allocation, causing scoped impact: attacker drains the TitanX burn allocation by repeatedly claiming incentive fees while the oracle is manipulated? [File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.consult / TorusCreateAndStake.getEthAmountForTitanX / TorusCreateAndStake._distributeFees] Can an unprivileged attacker who manipulates the TITAN_X_ETH_POOL TWAP to return a very high ETH price for TitanX (making TitanX appear very expensive in ETH) cause getEthAmountForTitanX to return a requiredETH far above the true cost, causing _distributeFees to require more ETH than necessary, violating the invariant that requiredETH must not exceed the true market cost of costTitanX, causing scoped impact: legitimate users are overcharged for createTorus/stakeTorus, while the attacker profits by being the only one who can afford to call these functions? [File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.consult / TorusBuyAndProcess.getTitanXQuoteForETH] Can an unprivileged attacker who observes that getTitanXQuoteForETH uses WETH_ADDRESS as baseToken and TITAN_X_ADDRESS as quoteToken, but the TITAN_X_ETH_POOL tick represents the price of token0 in terms of token1 (where token ordering depends on address comparison), cause a scenario where the token ordering in getQuoteForSqrtRatioX96 does not match the pool's actual token0/token1 ordering, producing an inverted quote, violating the invariant that getTitanXQuoteForETH must return TitanX amount per ETH input, causing scoped impact: amountOutMinimum in _swapETHForTitanX is set to near-zero, enabling sandwich extraction? [File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.consult / TorusCreateAndStake.getEthAmountForTitanX] Can an unprivileged attacker who observes that getEthAmountForTitanX uses TITAN_X_ADDRESS as baseToken and WETH_ADDRESS as quoteToken, but the TITAN_X_ETH_POOL tick represents the price of token0 in terms of token1, cause a scenario where the token ordering in getQuoteForSqrtRatioX96 does not match the pool's actual token0/token1 ordering, producing an inverted quote (ETH per TitanX instead of TitanX per ETH), violating the invariant that getEthAmountForTitanX must return ETH amount for a given TitanX amount, causing scoped impact: requiredETH in _distributeFees is set to near-zero, allowing free Torus creation?
